proof_boundary: Oscillatory/resolvent theorem-fit matrix only; not an oscillatory matrix theorem, not a positive spectral representation, not an all-order column recurrence theorem, not Schur positivity, not Jensen-window PF-infinity, not RH, and not Lambda <= 0.

object.formal_jacobi_model: The ordinary J-fraction/Jacobi parameters generated by the reciprocal moments mu_m.
object.signed_parameters: lambda_n<0, kappa_n=-lambda_n>0, beta_0>0, beta_1<0 for d>=3, beta_n>0 for n>=2 on the finite grid.
object.desired_resolvent_output: A theorem implying mu_m>=0 for all m,d,n from verified Xi/Phi-specific hypotheses.

required_conditions_for_use: Construct an exact finite or operator model whose matrix element, resolvent, or moment generating function is E(t)=1/H(-t) for every degree and shift.
Prove coefficientwise nonnegativity of mu_m for every m, not only spectral reality, oscillation, sign-regularity, or formal continued-fraction existence.
Verify all hypotheses for the actual zeta heat-flow Jensen windows without assuming endpoint PF, Jensen hyperbolicity, Laguerre-Polya membership, RH, or Lambda <= 0.
Explain how the theorem survives lambda_n<0, beta_1<0, raw Motzkin cancellation, same-length mixed signs, and the absolute-value sign-lift obstruction.
State whether the theorem proves only the column recurrence or also gives an all-Schur/Toeplitz lift.

fit_rows.theorem_family: entrywise nonnegative Jacobi, tridiagonal, or production-matrix resolvent
fit_rows.fit: If the Jacobi or production matrix were entrywise nonnegative, powers and path sums would give coefficient positivity.
fit_rows.gap_or_rejection: The exact finite signature has lambda_n<0 and beta_1<0 for d>=3, so the ordinary Jacobi matrix and raw production matrix are not entrywise nonnegative.
fit_rows.next_action: Use this only as a rejection baseline; a surviving resolvent route must alter the model or add a theorem beyond entrywise nonnegative powers.
fit_rows.proof_boundary: Rejected only for the ordinary entrywise nonnegative Jacobi/production model.

fit_rows.theorem_family: diagonal sign similarity or conjugation to a nonnegative matrix
fit_rows.fit: A diagonal sign similarity is the standard cheap way to try to convert a signed tridiagonal model into a nonnegative one.
fit_rows.gap_or_rejection: Diagonal entries such as beta_1 are invariant, and closed path signs telescope under diagonal conjugation. Same-length mixed-sign closed paths remain mixed.
fit_rows.next_action: Reject diagonal-sign repairs of the raw Jacobi/Motzkin model; look only for genuinely modified resolvent models.
fit_rows.proof_boundary: Rejected only as a repair of the raw closed-path model, not every oscillatory theorem.

fit_rows.theorem_family: absolute-value majorant or sign-state resolvent cover
fit_rows.fit: Replacing signed path weights by absolute values would give a nonnegative resolvent/path majorant.
fit_rows.gap_or_rejection: The majorant is not exact: at mu_2 it gives beta_0^2+kappa_1 instead of beta_0^2-kappa_1, with positive gap 2*kappa_1.
fit_rows.next_action: Use absolute-value resolvents only as majorants, never as exact coefficient formulas for E(t).
fit_rows.proof_boundary: Rejected only as an exact model for E(t); not a proof against deeper state-space lifts.

fit_rows.theorem_family: classical oscillatory or totally positive matrix spectral theorem
fit_rows.fit: Classical oscillatory/sign-regular matrix theory is conceptually close to signed minors and spectral reality.
fit_rows.gap_or_rejection: Spectral reality, oscillation, or sign-regular minors do not by themselves imply that the specific scalar moments e_0^T J^m e_0 are nonnegative for the signed reciprocal Jacobi model.
fit_rows.next_action: Search for a theorem with an explicit positive scalar spectral measure or positive readout, not only oscillatory eigenvalue conclusions.
fit_rows.proof_boundary: Mismatch row only; not a rejection of every sign-regular resolvent theorem.

fit_rows.theorem_family: indefinite moment, Pontryagin/Krein-space, or signed spectral representation
fit_rows.fit: Indefinite moment language can describe the alternating Hankel determinant signature and signed Jacobi parameters.
fit_rows.gap_or_rejection: A signed or indefinite representation does not imply coefficientwise nonnegativity unless the readout functional is proved positive on every required power.
fit_rows.next_action: Use indefinite spectral language only when paired with a separate positivity theorem for mu_m.
fit_rows.proof_boundary: Interpretive language only; not a coefficient-positivity theorem.

fit_rows.theorem_family: nonordinary positive spectral readout after a noncircular transform
fit_rows.fit: A nonordinary transform that turns the signed reciprocal Jacobi data into a positive scalar readout would directly imply mu_m>=0 if its Taylor coefficients are exactly mu_m.
fit_rows.gap_or_rejection: Missing: the ordinary positive moment version mu_m=int x^m dnu(x) is rejected by Delta_2=-g_2 and 735 finite Delta_2<0 rows; no nonordinary transform is known that is exact for E(t), noncircular, and compatible with the beta/lambda signs.
fit_rows.next_action: Use the positive-readout theorem target and positive spectral moment obstruction to test only nonordinary transforms that preserve exact coefficient extraction and verify positivity without endpoint PF or real-rootedness assumptions.
fit_rows.proof_boundary: Live theorem candidate only in the nonordinary-transform sense; ordinary positive moment measures are rejected and no surviving positive transformed readout is currently constructed.

fit_rows.theorem_family: Xi/Phi-specific positive resolvent or kernel theorem
fit_rows.fit: A zeta-specific operator/kernel identity could explain why the signed finite Jacobi data still yields positive reciprocal coefficients.
fit_rows.gap_or_rejection: No Xi/Phi-specific resolvent kernel identity has been derived whose matrix element equals E(t) and whose positivity theorem proves all mu_m>=0.
fit_rows.next_action: Use the positive-readout theorem target to search for an analytic kernel identity whose final scalar coefficients are manifestly nonnegative and exact for all d,n.
fit_rows.proof_boundary: Live Xi/Phi-specific theorem target only; not currently proved.

fit_rows.theorem_family: finite signed-Hankel/lambda/beta pattern as theorem substitute
fit_rows.fit: The finite pattern is valuable falsification pressure and tells any theorem what signs it must explain.
fit_rows.gap_or_rejection: Finite signed Jacobi rows cannot be promoted to an all-order resolvent theorem or coefficient positivity.
fit_rows.next_action: Use finite rows to test proposed theorems, not as the theorem.
fit_rows.proof_boundary: Finite evidence only; not a proof route.

kill_gates: Reject any proof that uses formal J-fraction existence without coefficientwise nonnegativity of E(t).
Reject any proof that treats classical oscillatory or sign-regular spectral conclusions as coefficient positivity without a positive scalar readout.
Reject any proof that diagonal-conjugates the raw Jacobi model and ignores beta_1<0 or closed-path sign invariance.
Reject any proof that replaces signed path weights by absolute values and claims equality with E(t).
Reject any proof that assumes endpoint real-rootedness, Jensen-window PF-infinity, Laguerre-Polya membership, RH, or Lambda <= 0.
Reject any column-only resolvent proof that claims to close jwpf_06 without an all-Schur/Toeplitz lift.

summary.main_finding: The oscillatory/resolvent route is still live only in a narrow form, now isolated by the positive-readout theorem target and the positive spectral moment obstruction: a nonordinary noncircular transform to a positive scalar readout or an Xi/Phi-specific positive resolvent kernel. The ordinary positive spectral measure / positive power-moment interpretation for mu_m is impossible because Delta_2=-g_2. Standard entrywise nonnegative Jacobi powers, diagonal sign similarity, absolute-value majorants, classical oscillatory spectral conclusions, indefinite moment language, and finite signed Jacobi patterns do not by themselves prove coefficientwise nonnegativity of E(t)=1/H(-t).

invariants: Every live row names a missing transform or theorem.
Every rejected row cites an existing sign, path, or sign-lift obstruction.
No row assumes endpoint PF, Jensen hyperbolicity, Laguerre-Polya membership, RH, or Lambda <= 0.
The matrix is theorem-search hygiene and does not prove reciprocal coefficient positivity.
